feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Ebola recoveries bring signs of hope in DRC as suspected cases emerge outside Africa
entry.summary: Four nurses who were being treated for ‌Ebola caused by the Bundibugyo strain of the virus have been discharged from a hospital in Bunia in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) after recovering from the disease, the World Health Organization (WHO) said on S…

entry.title: What Your Banana’s Color Says About Its Taste and Nutrition
entry.summary: See what the color of a banana says about its taste and nutrition, according to experts. While ripe bananas are higher in sugar, green bananas pack more fiber.

entry.title: Breakthrough Pill Nearly Doubles Survival Time For One of The Deadliest Cancers
entry.summary: WASHINGTON (AP) – A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.

entry.title: You Just Found Out You Have Chronic Kidney Disease—This Is What Experts Recommend You Do First
entry.summary: If you’ve recently been diagnosed with chronic kidney disease, don't panic. Here’s what kidney health experts recommend prioritizing.

entry.title: ‘Landmark’ pancreatic cancer drug keeps patients alive for twice as long
entry.summary: The highly anticipated results of a clinical trial for the pill, daraxonrasib, have been released, giving doctors hope for treating the stubbornly lethal disease.

entry.title: Brazil monitors two patients for possible Ebola infection
entry.summary: If confirmed, they would be the first infection cases outside Africa, since the outbreak began in DR Congo.

entry.title: Mysterious Origins of The Nicotine Molecule Are Finally Revealed
entry.summary: People have been consuming tobacco for millennia, though it wasn't until the late 1820s that nicotine was first extracted from tobacco plants.

entry.title: New cancer shot dissolves whole tumors in patients
entry.summary: Of the 102 participants, 43 saw a difference in the size of their tumors: some 28 saw their tumors shrink significantly, while 15 of the patients’ tumors dissolved altogether.

entry.title: 7 daily habits that can keep your teeth in tip-top shape
entry.summary: You might be surprised to know how oral health can affect the rest of your body. Here’s how to keep your teeth and gums in good shape.

entry.title: 311 complaint of the day: You're gonna need an ocean of calamine lotion on this Dorchester street
entry.summary: A concerned resident files a 311 complaint about the humongous outgrowth of poison ivy at Gallivan Boulevard and Neponset Avenue, by the expressway overpass in Dorchester's Neponset Circle: Read more.

entry.title: MAGE-A4/MAGE-A8-targeted TCR-based bispecific T cell engager in recurrent and/or refractory solid tumors: a phase 1 trial
entry.summary: As presented at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting, in a prespecified interim analysis of a phase 1a trial of IMA401—a new bispecific TCR-based T cell engager that binds a MAGE-A4/MAGE-A8 peptide presented by HLA-A*02:01—encouraging safety and a preliminary efficac…

entry.title: Africa CDC Says Over 1,100 Suspected Ebola Cases In DR Congo And Uganda
entry.summary: More than 1,100 people were suspected of having contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo and neighbouring Uganda, the head of the African Union's health agency said on Sunday in a commentary for the Financial Times.

entry.title: 4 Supplements to Take With Vitamin D for Better Absorption
entry.summary: Stacking certain supplements with Vitamin D can help boost absorption.

entry.title: Rabid Bat Found Near Sidewalk in Residential California Area Sparks Fears of Exposure to Fatal Disease
entry.summary: There are currently no known human or animal exposures associated with the incident on Tuesday, May 26

entry.title: Scientists found the hidden switch fueling alzheimer’s brain inflammation
entry.summary: Scientists at Scripps Research have uncovered a molecular “switch” that appears to fuel the damaging brain inflammation seen in Alzheimer’s disease. They found that a protein called STING becomes chemically altered in a way that keeps the brain’s immune syste…

entry.title: Kidney disease survivor finds hope through ministering brother’s organ donation
entry.summary: When the need for a kidney transplant came, Brad Bywater never expected to receive an organ donation from his ministering brother.

entry.title: The #1 Surprising Exercise for Reducing Visceral Fat, According to Experts
entry.summary: Find out why weight lifting is the best exercise for reducing visceral fat, according to experts. It's simple, customizable and can be done at home.

entry.title: Scientists Think They’ve Discovered How Humans Could Recover Lost Vision
entry.summary: Mammals aren’t known for the ocular regenerative powers, but a study shows that nature has a few tricks up its sleeve.

entry.title: Can Drinking Beet Juice Lower Blood Pressure In Older Adults? New Study Says Yes
entry.summary: High blood pressure is one of the most common health concerns among older adults. It increases the risk of heart disease, stroke, and other serious conditions.

entry.title: Brazil identifies 2 possible Ebola patients, as WHO reports some recoveries in Congo
entry.summary: The WHO said these five cases exemplify that recovery from the illness is possible, even without approved treatments or vaccines.

entry.title: Undigested fructose linked to anxiety and brain inflammation
entry.summary: A new study finds that an inability to properly digest fructose alters the gut microbiome, triggering low-grade inflammation. This digestive disruption may contribute to anxiety and depressive behaviors by alarming the brain's immune system.

entry.title: Two rupees for cancer: Inside India's smokeless tobacco epidemic that no one is stopping
entry.summary: India News: None of us are strangers to the fact that nicotine is bad. In India you cannot even go to the movies without a tobacco awareness ad playing. Your ciga.

entry.title: After new drug’s ‘unprecedented’ results for pancreatic cancer, doctors look at other uses
entry.summary: The experimental drug daraxonrasib, which doubled survival time in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, may also prove effective for lung, colon and ovarian cancers.

entry.title: Driver injured after avoiding hitting a deer in DeKalb County
entry.summary: The driver sustained a broken collarbone and was taken to a hospital for treatment. The car was heavily damaged, and the airbags were deployed.

entry.title: Mosquitoes learn to link the smell of DEET with a blood meal, new study finds
entry.summary: Mosquito repellents are key to protecting ourselves from mosquito bites and the pathogens they might carry. The most widely used active ingredient in insect repellents is N,N-diethyl-meta-toluamide, commonly known as DEET.

entry.title: The 1 Unexpected Sign Of Colon Cancer, According To Doctors
entry.summary: This symptom is often dismissed, which is why it's so important to recognize.

entry.title: 'Orthorexia' Is More And More Common. Here's What You Should Know About It.
entry.summary: Social media pressures only makes this condition more prevalent.

entry.title: Startup Testing Drugs on Freshly Extracted Human Brains That Are Kept On Life Support
entry.summary: The biotech startup Bexorg keeps the brains running for 24 hours before they're sliced apart and examined by scientists.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Some “Zombie Cells” May Actually Help You Live Longer
entry.summary: Scientists are discovering that some of the cells linked to aging may also be key to staying healthy.

entry.title: Intermittent fasting triggers surprising changes in the brain
entry.summary: Losing weight may involve rewiring the gut and the brain at the same time. In a study of obese adults, an intermittent fasting-style diet led to significant weight loss, healthier metabolic markers, and notable shifts in gut bacteria. Brain scans also reveale…

entry.title: Parents Learn They ‘Unknowingly Carried’ Gene Mutation After Both Kids Are Diagnosed with Rare, Often Fatal Disorder (Exclusive)
entry.summary: “In so many ways, [my children] are pioneers, and I could not be prouder of them,” Jillian Arnold tells PEOPLE

entry.title: How 5 minutes of movement can change your life : The Sunday Story
entry.summary: Scientists say that sitting is the new smoking. The average American adult now sits for 9-10 hours per day. What’s the least amount of movement someone can do to offset the harms of our modern sedentary lifestyle? Manoush Zomorodi, host of NPR’s TED Radio Hou…

entry.title: Women hope renaming PCOS health condition could help millions of women worldwide
entry.summary: Rochelle Lewis is among more than 170 million woman globally to be diagnosed with PCOS.

entry.title: How Nigeria’s ‘algorithmic apothecary’ fuels a surge in risky herbal cures
entry.summary: Unverified herbal remedies promoted on social media are driving rising health risks and delayed treatment in Nigeria.

entry.title: Former PPD Officer Facing Two Additional Charges Of Failing To Disclose HIV Status To Partner
entry.summary: Local online newspaper for North Escambia County Florida, Pensacola, Walnut Hill, Bratt, McDavid, Molino, Century, Cantonment, Atmore, Flomaton, News

entry.title: Barre is one of the best types of exercise you can do to promote healthy aging, says an expert instructor—start with these three moves
entry.summary: This beginner-friendly home routine is even suitable for the over-70s

entry.title: More than 300 sickened after Salmonella outbreak confirmed at New York correctional facility
entry.summary: Inmates began reporting symptoms to medical staff on May 25.

entry.title: I’ve Tried Every Mosquito Trick—This Is The Only Thing I Keep Buying
entry.summary: These citronella incense sticks are the secret to bug-free porch dinners.

entry.title: How a dose of medicinal cannabis alters brain waves during sleep
entry.summary: A new clinical trial mapped the sleeping brains of people with insomnia after they took a cannabis-based drug. The treatment altered deeper sleep stages and reduced dreaming, though participants experienced no cognitive impairment the morning after.

entry.title: 4 High-Fat Foods You Should Be Eating to Lower High Blood Pressure, According to Experts
entry.summary: Supporting healthy blood pressure doesn’t mean you need to cut all fat from your diet. Learn about four fatty foods that can benefit heart health and blood pressure.

entry.title: Giant Study Reveals The Secret to Heart Health Isn't Low-Carb or Low-Fat
entry.summary: The key to heart health isn't cutting down on pasta or potatoes, new evidence suggests; it's not even a low-fat diet.

entry.title: Do you need SPF 30, 50 or 100? Dermatologists answer your burning sunscreen questions
entry.summary: PolitiFact asked board-certified dermatologists to answer people's burning questions about SPF. When it comes to sunscreen, they advised buying broad-spectrum sunscreen that’s SPF 30 or higher and reapplying regularly.

entry.title: For Gen Z, 'Tanmaxxing' Rules, Despite Skin Cancer Warnings
entry.summary: Myths proliferating on social media, low cancer fears are fueling risky tanning trends

entry.title: Keto Diet May Help Protect Against Multiple Brain Diseases, Study Finds
entry.summary: A diet designed for weight loss could offer a different bonus benefit, according to a new review.

entry.title: Nanofiber implant delivers three drugs, doubles survival in glioblastoma mice
entry.summary: Researchers with the University of Cincinnati and Johns Hopkins Medicine developed a potential treatment for brain cancer that uses nanofibers embedded with a combination of drugs that work in concert to target tumors. The drugs proved more effective in combi…

entry.title: Cannabis linked to a surprising change in testosterone levels
entry.summary: A study of young men found that cannabis users had higher testosterone, DHT, and other testicular hormones than non users.

entry.title: Rabid bat found in residential area triggers warning from police
entry.summary: A rabid bat found near a Bay Area residential neighborhood has sparked a warning from the police about the dangerous exposure to humans and pets.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Body When You Stop Eating Sugar, According to Nutrition Experts
entry.summary: Cutting back on added sugar can improve energy, sleep, mood, gut health, and blood sugar. We spoke with dietitians to help reduce it sustainably.

entry.title: The unpredictable disease attacking women 5 times more than men — doctors only study it for ‘one minute’
entry.summary: The condition is elusive and shifts without warning. Patients eat normally one day, then go into anaphylaxis the next. Reactions can be triggered by stress,...

entry.title: Man Can't Work Because He Gets Drunk Without Drinking
entry.summary: Rare gut condition leaves Nova Scotia father frequently, unpredictably intoxicated
